deviation_from_activation_manager.standard_flow_summary: Activation Manager normally prepares refinement, signature, packet hydration, branch/worktree prep, task-board, traceability, build-order, and readiness before execution.
deviation_from_activation_manager.approved_operator_deviation: Operator requested a hotfix copy of an active worktree and ad hoc microtasks to preserve momentum while keeping state recoverable.
deviation_from_activation_manager.control_kept: Use a real git worktree and branch, gov-kernel junction, WP packet, task-board entry, traceability entry, build-order sync, WP communications, and one MT contract per operator-requested product edit.

worktree_policy.copy_strategy: created real git worktree at source HEAD via worktree helper, then overlaid source dirty/untracked product files while excluding .git and .GOV

microtask_policy.rule: Before any product-code edit for a requested change, create or update one MT-### contract under this WP and set active_mt_id in mt_index.json.
allowed_without_mt: task-board, traceability, build-order, and communication pointer setup
microtask_policy.handoff_rule: Each MT records target files, expected behavior, validation commands, risk notes, and closeout state before the next MT begins.

parallel_agent_policy.constraint: Parallel subagents may inspect or implement independent MTs only after the MT owns a non-overlapping file set or explicit merge plan.
parallel_agent_policy.state_recovery: Subagents must write results back to MT contracts or WP communications, not to chat-only memory.

last_state_update.summary: MT-039 reached ready-for-validation after focused headless CKC linked-media panel/backend mock roundtrip proof, 5-pass repeat, and two adversarial review lenses passed. No new MT was activated.

lifecycle.claim_notes: Operator requested setup and future ad hoc MT execution in a hotfix copy of the active native editors worktree.

scope.base_scope: Overhaul CastKit Codex and PoseKit inside Handshake Atelier as native, interconnected Handshake features, starting with non-Rust language reduction before visual and behavioral parity work.
scope.current_phase: Recoverable MT backlog and active implementation for native Atelier/CKC/Posekit/Ingest/Argus overhaul.
scope.later_phase: Visual and behavioral overhaul to mirror the original proven test app inside Handshake while preserving Handshake's native interconnected model.
in_scope: Atelier CKC/PoseKit native Rust backend and native UI surfaces
Legacy TypeScript/React or JavaScript CKC/PoseKit surfaces that need migration, replacement, or containment
Pose artifact contracts, receipts, and capability wiring used by other Handshake modules
Feature parity checks against the original CKC/PoseKit test app before each behavioral change
non_goals: Do not edit ../handshake_main directly for this WP.
Do not copy .git or .GOV from the source worktree.
Do not merge, validate PASS/FAIL, or close the WP from Kernel Builder alone.
Do not reintroduce SQLite, Electron IPC authority, localhost-only app assumptions, or direct external LLM control patterns into Handshake runtime.

patterns_found: CKC/PoseKit logic already has Rust Atelier domain code, but legacy TSX/JS surfaces still exist.
Handshake worktree creation should use the governed helper to preserve the .GOV junction and avoid inherited governance dirt.
A native feature overhaul should compare against the original test app behavior before replacing surfaces.
rejected_options: Raw filesystem copy of the source worktree, because copying .git would corrupt isolation and copying .GOV would risk following the junction.
Full Activation Manager packet ceremony for every tweak, because the operator requested an ad hoc hotfix lane with recoverable MT state instead.
research_basis.selected_approach: Real hotfix git worktree from source HEAD, dirty product overlay, JSON-first WP contract, MT-per-change execution, and minimal shared record updates.

risks.risk: Inherited WP-KERNEL-012 dirty files can pollute CKC commits or reviews.
risks.failure_scenario: A CKC MT stages all files and accidentally ships unrelated diagnostics or Palmistry work from the copied source baseline.
risks.mitigation: Each MT must record touched files and use path-limited staging/review. Inherited baseline files remain explicitly listed here.

risks.risk: Feature parity drifts from the original test app.
risks.failure_scenario: Native Rust rewrites remove behaviors that made the test app high-value in production.
risks.mitigation: Before behavioral or visual changes, create an MT that records original-app behavior evidence and parity expectations.

risks.risk: Non-Rust removal can become language-churn instead of product value.
risks.failure_scenario: A TSX or JS surface is removed before an equivalent native Handshake route exists.
risks.mitigation: Inventory first, then migrate surface-by-surface only when replacement behavior, receipts, and tests are identified.

risks.risk: Parallel agents conflict in the same files.
risks.failure_scenario: Two MTs edit the native shell or Atelier panel concurrently and produce a hard-to-recover merge conflict.
risks.mitigation: Parallel work requires non-overlapping MT target files or an explicit merge owner in mt_index.json.

risks.failure_scenario: After leaving repo governance, a no-context model cannot determine active WP, active MT, worktree, branch, source baseline, or validation state.
risks.mitigation: Keep packet.json, mt_index.json, task board, traceability, build-order, and WP communications synced after each MT.

minimum_controls: One MT-### contract per operator-requested product change.
No product edits outside ../wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul for this WP.
No commit or validation claim that includes inherited baseline files unless the MT explicitly owns them.
Record original-app parity evidence before visual or behavioral overhaul MTs.

roi_additions.addition: Create a CKC/PoseKit non-Rust surface inventory as the first implementation MT.
roi_additions.why_high_roi: It is cheap now and prevents blind language churn.
roi_additions.gap_closed: Clarifies which TSX/JS surfaces are legacy, still active, or replaceable.
roi_additions.reuses: Existing rg/code search and Atelier/native module files.
roi_additions.validation: Inventory MT cites every non-Rust CKC/PoseKit surface and proposed disposition.

roi_additions.addition: Add an original-app parity checklist before visual/behavioral changes.
roi_additions.why_high_roi: The source app already proved production value; preserving its behavior is the main value driver.
roi_additions.gap_closed: Prevents native rewrite drift from the proven workflow.
roi_additions.reuses: Original test app files and Handshake Atelier packet scope.
roi_additions.validation: Each parity item maps to code paths, UI behavior, or a deliberate non-goal.

roi_additions.addition: Keep MT receipts path-limited with touched-file declarations.
roi_additions.why_high_roi: It makes parallel agent work safer while the baseline contains unrelated inherited changes.
roi_additions.reuses: MT contract files and WP communications receipts.
roi_additions.validation: Diff review compares MT declared files to actual git status/diff.

tooling_notes.note: Repomem decision writes failed with 'No active session' after open; this WP should not depend on repomem active-session state until that tooling gap is repaired.
A broad .GOV rg search timed out; subsequent targeted file reads were used instead.
Two PowerShell Select-Object range reads initially used a string range; corrected with parenthesized range syntax.
WP communications initially rejected the packet until the workflow tuple and canonical communication paths were declared; ensure-wp-communications now passes.

Repo-wide just gov-check exceeded a 120 second timeout and left Node child processes; only the gov-check-related PIDs were stopped. Targeted checks for this WP passed: build-order-sync idempotent, wp-communication-health-check STATUS ok, and wp-declared-topology-check PASS.

last_state_update.summary: MT-039 product patch is ready for validation with focused headless CKC linked-media panel/backend mock roundtrip proof. No active MT is selected; do not start a new MT without operator direction.
